changes_made: Documented that nano-pdf CLI only supports edit/add, not merge/extract/count
Added Python workflow using pdfminer (extract_text) and pypdf (PdfReader/PdfWriter)
Added explicit instruction to ignore LSP false-positive errors for pdfminer/pypdf
Emphasized writing .py files instead of python3 -c one-liners (for loops cause syntax errors)
Added guidance to never hardcode values and always parse actual PDF text
Provided step-by-step workflow: extract text first, print it, then parse it
Showed correct merge pattern using PdfWriter (not deprecated PdfMerger)
persistent_issues: qwen3-30b frequently reads PDFs with the read tool (binary content) instead of using pdfminer as instructed
Model gets stuck in loops when LSP reports errors, trying to fix imports instead of running the script
Model sometimes hardcodes/hallucinates data values instead of parsing extracted text
Model struggles with Python syntax (missing closing braces/brackets in dicts/lists)
pdf-text-extract task consistently fails because the model doesn't follow the two-step extract-then-parse workflow
